content: Oh, well, during that dinner, I guess I just felt kind of stuck there. I wanted to say something interesting or maybe contribute my own idea, but then I froze and just kept smiling and nodding, even though I was getting tired of it. Laughing too much made me feel like I wasn’t good enough, and I ended up agreeing to things that weren’t really important to me. It’s exhausting trying to hide my discomfort when everyone else seems so interested. I just don’t want them to think I’m not interesting or worth being around. It’s like I’m always being pushed away because I don’t seem special or unique enough. I wish I could change that feeling, but it’s hard to stop feeling like I’m already out of place. It’s frustrating because I know I should be able to connect better with people if I try. So, I just keep pretending to be okay, even though I’m scared of disappointing anyone. I appreciate you playing the part of patient, though. It helps me imagine what it would be like to experience those kinds of situations firsthand. Just knowing I’m not going to make it right doesn’t help much. Thank you for being such a good friend though—without you, I wouldn’t know where to start. I’ll miss this moment. Thanks for asking. I’ll try again soon. Please let me know if anything I said isn’t quite right. I love you, and I hope you’re doing well. Thanks for being here, thank you so much for letting me experience this. I appreciate you, too. I really need some advice. What do you think I can do differently next time? I’d really like to learn from you how to be more open and less afraid to admit my feelings. That way, I can feel more accepted and understood. Thanks again for being here. I’ll be back later. Love you! I

Yeah, I guess I’ve been feeling really stuck lately. When my partner’s gone off to work, I feel like maybe we’re just not meant to be together anymore. It’s like they’re allured away by some other thing happening, and then they just start ignoring me. I keep thinking, “Why bother staying? Why even try when they won’t let me in?” It makes me feel so helpless, like I’m failing to hold onto anything special. Sometimes I just end up shutting down and pretending things aren’t too bad, which doesn’t always help. I wish there was some way to say, “I need some space,” or that they’d come back sooner than they planned. It’s hard to know how to stop worrying so much about them getting upset or not wanting to be around me. I hope someone can see what’s going on inside me—because I feel like no one understands or cares enough about what I’m going through.

I hope they’ll let me take on this one instead of just dismissing me completely. It’s frustrating, but I don’t really have a clue how to change things. But then I start doubting myself again, and it feels like no matter what I do, I’m still just going to fall further behind. So, any advice would be super appreciated! Thanks so much for asking. I really need some clarity here. I’ll try to push harder, but I’m not sure I can keep going. I’m sorry I didn’t tell you sooner. I just don’t trust my own judgment anymore. It’s like I’m scared of facing the truth about why I’m not strong enough for this job. But I know it’s time to stop letting it hold me back. Please help me make this decision now.
